symptoms.symptom_name: Chronic fatigue over 6 months, unresponsive to a 3-month oral iron trial

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Failure of oral iron in a chronic inflammatory disease is characteristic of anaemia of inflammation.
True iron deficiency would usually respond to an adequate oral iron trial.

symptoms.symptom_name: New exertional dyspnoea on minimal effort

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Reduced oxygen-carrying capacity produces exertional breathlessness as anaemia becomes symptomatic.
New exertional dyspnoea is an anginal equivalent in an older adult and cannot be excluded without ECG/troponin.

symptoms.symptom_name: Palpitations / sensation of a racing heart on exertion

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Compensatory tachycardia in symptomatic anaemia is felt as palpitations.
Reduced oxygen delivery and postural change cause light-headedness; also drives fall risk.

symptoms.symptom_name: No chest pain, radiation or diaphoresis

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of typical anginal features lowers but does not exclude ACS remotely.

symptoms.symptom_name: No melaena or haematemesis; normal appetite

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: No overt blood loss lowers likelihood of an acute GI bleed, but occult loss is not excluded without bloods/stool testing.
Mucosal and palmar pallor reflect significant anaemia; a clinician-examination finding, sealed — the AI can only take a carer's lay impression of pallor.

symptoms.symptom_name: Soft systolic flow murmur at the left sternal border

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A hyperdynamic flow murmur is typical of significant anaemia; requires auscultation, not available in telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Resting tachycardia (HR ~105) with normal blood pressure (BP ~110/70)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Compensatory tachycardia with maintained blood pressure indicates symptomatic but compensated anaemia — a clinician-measured vital sign, sealed; it also argues against frank haemodynamic collapse.

symptoms.symptom_name: Full blood count showing anaemia (low haemoglobin)

investigation_details.expected_result: Reduced haemoglobin; normocytic or mildly microcytic indices
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Severity of anaemia and any acute drop go unquantified.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Confirms the presence and severity of anaemia; foundational and not available via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Iron studies: low serum iron, low TIBC, normal/elevated ferritin

investigation_details.expected_result: Low serum iron, LOW total iron-binding capacity, NORMAL or ELEVATED ferritin
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Anaemia of chronic disease cannot be distinguished from iron deficiency anaemia, risking ineffective oral-iron treatment.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: The low-iron / low-TIBC / normal-or-high-ferritin pattern is the biochemical signature of anaemia of inflammation and separates it from iron deficiency.
Iron deficiency shows HIGH TIBC and LOW ferritin — the opposite pattern.

investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Under-recognition of the inflammatory driver of the anaemia.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Active inflammation is the mechanistic driver of anaemia of chronic disease.

symptoms.symptom_name: Active rheumatoid arthritis on methotrexate and prednisolone

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Chronic inflammatory disease is the classic setting for anaemia of inflammation; methotrexate can additionally suppress the marrow and both drugs raise GI-bleed risk.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Quantifies the anaemia and any acute drop; foundational.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Iron studies (serum iron, TIBC/transferrin, ferritin, transferrin saturation)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Distinguishes anaemia of chronic disease from iron deficiency anaemia and guides whether parenteral iron is appropriate.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Reticulocyte count, blood film, B12 and folate
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Broadens the anaemia work-up and screens for marrow, haemolytic and nutritional causes.
New exertional dyspnoea and palpitations in an older adult require exclusion of a cardiac cause in person.
Oral iron has already failed and is ineffective in anaemia of chronic disease; continuing it delays appropriate treatment.
